content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

Accept amendment fr-6-0736c40d: it identifies a real precedence regression in backend resolution. Reject amendment fr-6-013a5281: code paths for default final-review panel resolution already default to Codex xhigh + Claude opus, and the cited run/journal snapshot is driven by an explicit project override, not unspecified defaults.

## Votes

- `fr-6-0736c40d`: Accept
  `default_backend_runtime_settings` now seeds Codex role-models for `implementer` and `final_reviewer`, and `resolve_backend_selection` for implementer falls back to `default_implementer_backend()` (Codex) while `target_for_family` prioritizes role model maps before base model. This makes `default_model` on `default_backend = "codex"` no longer affect implementer/final reviewer resolution, so explicit `default_model` overrides regress as described.
- `fr-6-013a5281`: Reject
  Current defaults already specify `default_final_review_backends = [codex, claude]` and resolution uses role models (`final_reviewer` from Codex role model, Claudes default model id). The provided `run.json`/`journal.ndjson` entries show `codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh`, matching explicit project settings in `.git/ralph-burning-live/projects/utm/config.toml` (`final_review.backends = ["codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh", "codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh"]`), not unspecified-default behavior, so this is not a default-resolution defect.
